lesson.menu_desc: Write Clear Corporate Emails -- Opening, Body & Closing -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 3 -- Email Essentials -- Opening, Body & Closing
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Simone Quiles de Santana Marques | Aula 3 -- Email Essentials

listenings.text: Hi Simone, this is Thomas Reed from outside counsel. I just sent you an email regarding the non-disclosure agreement. The subject line is NDA, Draft for Review, and you will find the draft as an attachment. In the body, I asked you to check two points: the term length in clause two and the governing law in clause five. Please reply to me directly, and feel free to forward my email to anyone on your team who should read it. If you add a colleague, just put them in CC so we all stay informed. I will follow up on Thursday if I do not hear back. Thank you, and I look forward to your reply.

Hi team, this is Simone. I am leaving a quick note after reviewing the email from Thomas Reed regarding the NDA. A few reminders about our emails this week. First, always use a clear subject line so the recipient knows the topic at once. Second, open with a polite greeting and state your reason in the first line. Third, keep the body short, with one clear request per email. When you reply, do not forget the attachment if you mention one. Please put me in CC on anything sent to outside counsel, and forward Thomas's draft to the contracts team. I will sign off for today, but follow up with me tomorrow if you have any questions. Thank you, and have a good evening.

extra_audio.text: Thomas sends Simone an email with the NDA draft as an attachment. Simone reads the clear subject line and the polite greeting. Simone replies and asks Thomas to review two clauses. Simone forwards the email to her team and adds a colleague in CC. Thomas follows up on Thursday, and they sign off.
